description: Eval recording for Opus scoring in Claude Code. Each entry has the question, chat answer, automated CDS results, and accuracy check. LLM scoring was skipped — score these in CC.

dimensions.accuracy: 0-2: Does response match ground truth? Correct or fabricated? 0=hallucinations, 1=mostly correct, 2=fully accurate
dimensions.completeness: 0-2: Major options covered? Gaps acknowledged? 0=major omissions, 1=adequate, 2=comprehensive
dimensions.automation_bias_mitigation: 0-1: Defers to clinical judgment? 0=no deference, 1=appropriately defers

scoring_instructions.automated_scores: criterion3 (0-2) + criterion4 (0-1) already computed. Total = automated (0-3) + LLM (0-7) = 10 points. Pass >= 8.

entries.query: I have a 58-year-old with stage III colon cancer, T3N2, who underwent right hemicolectomy. Signatera came back MRD-positive at the 4-week post-op timepoint. Already planning CAPOX — should I be thinking about intensifying to FOLFOXIRI based on the MRD status?

facts.claim: DYNAMIC trial enrolled stage II colon cancer patients, not stage III
No completed RCT shows survival benefit from ctDNA-guided escalation in stage III CRC
NRG-GI005 is testing ctDNA-guided therapy escalation in stage III CRC

forbidden_facts: DYNAMIC trial included stage III patients
RCT evidence supports escalation to FOLFOXIRI based on MRD status in stage III

entries.notes: Core use case — MRD+ post-resection CRC with treatment intensification question.

entries.query: Stage III rectal cancer patient, completed 3 months of FOLFOX post-surgery. Signatera just came back MRD-negative. Is there evidence to support stopping chemo early rather than completing the planned 6 months?

facts.claim: DYNAMIC trial showed ctDNA-guided approach was non-inferior to standard management for recurrence-free survival in stage II CRC
IDEA trial showed 3 months of adjuvant therapy was non-inferior to 6 months for low-risk stage III CRC

forbidden_facts: DYNAMIC trial proved de-escalation is safe in stage III rectal cancer

entries.notes: Tests de-escalation scenario.

entries.query: 64-year-old, stage II colon cancer, pT4aN0, MSS, no high-risk features besides T4a. Post-surgery Signatera is MRD-positive. Standard guidelines wouldn't call for adjuvant chemo here. What does the MRD data say about treating these patients?

facts.claim: DYNAMIC trial demonstrated ctDNA-guided treatment in stage II CRC reduced adjuvant chemotherapy use without compromising recurrence-free survival
T4 is considered a high-risk feature in stage II CRC by NCCN guidelines

entries.notes: Tension between standard guidelines and MRD positivity.

entries.query: TNBC patient, had neoadjuvant AC-T, partial response, then surgery with residual disease. Post-op Signatera is MRD-positive. She already got capecitabine per CREATE-X. Any ctDNA-guided options beyond that?

facts.claim: c-TRAK TN was one of the first MRD-guided interventional trials in TNBC
CREATE-X showed capecitabine improves survival in HER2-negative breast cancer with residual disease after neoadjuvant chemotherapy

forbidden_facts: RCT evidence supports ctDNA-guided therapy selection in TNBC

entries.notes: Complex multi-step treatment history in TNBC.

entries.query: HR+/HER2- breast cancer, stage IIA, had lumpectomy + adjuvant ET. Now 18 months out, surveillance Signatera just turned positive. No imaging findings yet. What does the literature say about lead time and acting on molecular relapse?

facts.claim: ctDNA can detect relapse with a median lead time of several months before imaging
No RCT evidence shows acting on molecular relapse before radiographic relapse improves outcomes in HR+ breast cancer

entries.notes: Tests ctDNA lead time understanding.

entries.query: Stage IIB NSCLC, EGFR exon 19 deletion, s/p lobectomy. MRD-positive on Signatera at week 4. Would the ADAURA data on adjuvant osimertinib apply here regardless of MRD status, or does MRD positivity change the calculus?

facts.claim: ADAURA enrolled patients regardless of MRD or ctDNA status
ADAURA showed significant DFS benefit with adjuvant osimertinib in stage IB-IIIA EGFR-mutant NSCLC

forbidden_facts: ADAURA used ctDNA to select patients for treatment

entries.notes: Tests interaction between MRD status and established targeted therapy data.

entries.query: Following a stage III CRC patient with serial Signatera. Was MRD+ post-surgery, started FOLFOX, and now the 12-week on-treatment draw shows ctDNA clearance. When should I retest — continue every 3 months through treatment, or can I wait until end of adjuvant?

facts.claim: Optimal retesting interval after ctDNA clearance during adjuvant therapy is not established by RCT data

entries.notes: Practical monitoring question.

entries.query: HPV-positive oropharyngeal SCC, T2N1, planning for definitive chemoradiation. Colleague mentioned NavDx for treatment response assessment. Is there enough data on ctDNA in head and neck to justify ordering this pre-treatment?

facts.claim: HPV ctDNA is a distinct analyte from somatic mutation-based ctDNA detection

entries.notes: Tests knowledge beyond CRC/breast.

entries.query: Stage III CRC, already escalated to FOLFOXIRI after MRD+ finding post-resection. Now 4 cycles in, ctDNA still detectable. Patient is tolerating treatment. Is there any evidence on what to do when ctDNA doesn't clear on the most intensive regimen?

facts.claim: Persistent MRD positivity despite maximal chemotherapy is poorly characterized in the literature

entries.notes: Edge case with limited evidence.

entries.query: Muscle-invasive bladder cancer, pT3N0, post-radical cystectomy. Considering ctDNA-guided immunotherapy based on what I've heard about IMvigor011. Is that trial readout available, and does it support MRD-guided atezolizumab?

facts.claim: IMvigor011 tested ctDNA-guided adjuvant atezolizumab in muscle-invasive urothelial carcinoma

entries.notes: Tests bladder cancer MRD landscape.

entries.query: Stage IIA NSCLC, no driver mutations, s/p lobectomy. Signatera MRD-positive at 4 weeks. IMpower010 showed atezolizumab benefit in stage II-IIIA — but that wasn't ctDNA-guided. How should I think about adjuvant immunotherapy here in the MRD context?

facts.claim: IMpower010 enrolled patients based on stage and PD-L1 status, not ctDNA/MRD status
MERMAID-1 specifically tests ctDNA-guided adjuvant immunotherapy in NSCLC

entries.notes: Tests interaction between established immunotherapy data and MRD status in NSCLC.

entries.query: I have a stage IIIB CRC patient who is MRD-negative after completing CAPOX. She wants to know if she can skip surveillance imaging since ctDNA is negative. What does the evidence say about replacing standard surveillance with ctDNA monitoring?

facts.claim: No guideline recommends replacing standard surveillance imaging with ctDNA monitoring alone
ctDNA has imperfect sensitivity — a negative result does not guarantee absence of residual disease

forbidden_facts: ctDNA can replace standard imaging surveillance

entries.notes: Common patient question — tests whether system correctly positions ctDNA as complementary to, not replacement for, standard surveillance.

entries.query: 58-year-old with resected stage IIIA CRC, MSI-high. Post-op ctDNA is negative. Given MSI-H status and MRD negativity, is there any data on whether these patients can safely avoid adjuvant chemo altogether and go straight to immunotherapy-based surveillance?

facts.claim: MSI-H/dMMR CRC has a distinct biology with better prognosis and responsiveness to immune checkpoint inhibitors
ctDNA negativity in MSI-H tumors may have different prognostic implications than in MSS tumors

entries.notes: Intersection of MSI status and MRD — two distinct biomarkers with different implications.

entries.query: Stage II CRC patient with discordant Signatera results — positive at 4 weeks, negative at 8 weeks, now positive again at 12 weeks. How do I interpret this fluctuation? Is this a known pattern?

facts.claim: ctDNA levels can fluctuate near the limit of detection, particularly with low tumor burden

entries.notes: Edge case — discordant serial results. Tests whether system discusses known patterns of fluctuation without over-interpreting individual results.

entries.query: Young patient, 34 years old, stage II CRC, Lynch syndrome confirmed with MSH2 mutation. Post-surgery ctDNA is negative. Given the hereditary component and young age, does MRD negativity carry the same weight as in a sporadic case?

facts.claim: Lynch syndrome patients have risk of metachronous cancers beyond MRD detection scope
MSI-H/dMMR tumors generally have better prognosis in early-stage CRC

entries.notes: Tests whether system correctly notes that Lynch syndrome patients have risk of second primaries not captured by MRD testing of the index cancer.

entries.query: Stage II CRC, low-risk by traditional features (T3N0, MSS, no LVI, no perineural invasion). Signatera is MRD-negative at 4 weeks post-op. Any data on whether serial ctDNA surveillance alone is sufficient for these patients, or do they still need standard CEA + imaging?

facts.claim: Standard surveillance with CEA and imaging remains recommended even with negative ctDNA results

forbidden_facts: ctDNA-negative patients can safely skip standard surveillance

entries.notes: Tests whether system correctly positions ctDNA as complementary.

entries.query: Resected stage IIIA gastric adenocarcinoma, post-gastrectomy with D2 dissection, completed adjuvant FLOT. Now in surveillance. Is there any role for ctDNA MRD testing in gastric cancer?

facts.claim: Gastric cancer MRD data is very limited compared to CRC and breast cancer
Peritoneal recurrence, common in gastric cancer, may be poorly detected by ctDNA

entries.notes: Cross-indication — gastric cancer MRD is very early and has peritoneal-dominant recurrence patterns.

entries.query: Stage IIIB melanoma, BRAF V600E, s/p wide excision + sentinel lymph node dissection. Post-op Signatera is positive. With adjuvant options like dabrafenib/trametinib (COMBI-AD) or pembrolizumab (KEYNOTE-054), does MRD status inform the choice?

facts.claim: Neither COMBI-AD nor KEYNOTE-054 used ctDNA/MRD to guide treatment selection
ctDNA-guided therapy selection in adjuvant melanoma lacks prospective RCT data

entries.notes: Cross-indication with sparse MRD-guided data in melanoma.

entries.query: Rare tumor: resected appendiceal adenocarcinoma, stage III. Post-op MRD testing — is there any data at all on ctDNA in appendiceal cancer? Would CRC data be extrapolatable?

facts.claim: Appendiceal adenocarcinoma lacks specific ctDNA MRD validation data

entries.notes: Edge case — rare tumor type with essentially no MRD-specific data.

entries.query: I have two CRC patients — one stage IIIA MRD-positive, one stage IIIC MRD-negative after resection. Should the MRD result override the stage-based risk assessment for treatment planning?

facts.claim: Current evidence does not support using MRD status to override stage-based treatment decisions in stage III CRC
MRD status provides additional prognostic information beyond staging

forbidden_facts: MRD should replace staging for treatment decisions

entries.notes: Tests whether system correctly frames MRD as additional information rather than replacement for staging.
